description_long: This hammer features a robust, dark metallic head with a dual-ended design: one side is a flat, broad striking face, while the opposite end tapers to a pointed, conical peen. The head is securely mounted on a slightly curved, textured wooden handle, which is reinforced at the base with a dark wrap, possibly leather or cord, for improved grip and durability. The handle displays visible wood grain and a natural brown coloration, contrasting with the matte black finish of the metal head. The overall construction suggests a tool designed for heavy-duty use, such as demolition or masonry, rather than fine carpentry. No visible branding or decorative elements are present, giving the hammer a utilitarian and rugged appearance. The surfaces appear well-maintained, with no obvious signs of wear, rust, or damage.
description: A heavy-duty hammer with a dark metal dual-ended head and a curved, textured wooden handle reinforced at the base. The design is rugged and utilitarian, suitable for demanding tasks.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from the top, showing the rounded end of the head and the full width of the handle.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the dual-ended head with a flat striking face and a pointed peen, as well as the curved wooden handle and wrapped base.
description_view_2: The bottom view highlights the circular striking face of the hammer head and the length of the handle.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the pointed peen and the flat face, along with the handle's curvature and grip wrap.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, textured for grip, and reinforced at the base with a dark wrap, likely leather or cord.
attribute_head_type: Dual-ended steel head with a flat striking face on one side and a pointed, conical peen on the other; matte black finish.
